description:
GOAL: Inter-process messaging. send(pid, msg) and receive primitives. v1 is COPY-ONLY (the zero-copy fast path comes in .19.5).

## Decisions captured

- Each process owns a Vec<FzValue> mailbox (FIFO).
- send(pid, msg) deep-copies msg into receiver's heap (walks the FzValue closure-of-reachability and reallocates in receiver's heap), then enqueues into receiver's mailbox.
- receive blocks the current process until a matching message arrives. v1: receive returns the next message (no pattern selectivity; user does match at fz level).
- When send delivers to a Blocked process, the scheduler marks it Ready.
- Selective receive with pattern matching is a follow-up.
- Receive with timeout is a follow-up.

## Deliverables

- src/runtime/mailbox.rs: VecDeque-backed mailbox, push_back from send, pop_front from receive.
- Deep-copy walker: traverses FzValue closure, reallocs heap objects in target heap. Handles all HeapKinds (Struct, List, Bitstring, Vec*, Map). Cycles via mark-set during walk.
- Builtins: send/2, receive/0 (and receive/1 for timeout if we want it for v1; defer).
- Scheduler integration: send wakes Blocked receiver; receive yields if mailbox empty.

## Tests

- Spawn process B; A sends [1, 2, 3] to B; B receives and prints. Both processes' heaps see independent copies.
- Send a tuple of mixed values.
- Send a deeply nested structure (list of maps of bitstrings) — deep copy correctness.
- Mailbox FIFO ordering preserved.
- Receive blocks correctly (process state becomes Blocked).

## Risks

- Deep-copy must handle all heap kinds correctly, including cycles. Mitigation: explicit forwarding-pointer technique during copy (mark each src object with its dst pointer to handle cycles + sharing).
- Performance: deep copy is the v1 cost; .19.5 fixes hot cases via transferable arenas.

## Out of scope

- Selective receive (follow-up)
- Receive timeout (follow-up)
- Zero-copy send fast path (.19.5)
title: Mailbox + send / receive (copy fallback)
